response:
I don’t have any meeting notes or documents in front of me, so I can’t tell you which customers were discussed for Project Sentinel yet. If you share the meeting notes, transcript, email thread, or tell me where the discussion happened (Slack channel, Confluence page, Salesforce opportunity, date of meeting), I’ll extract the customer list and summarize follow-ups.

In the meantime, here are quick, practical ways you can find the customers discussed and some ready-to-run queries/searches you can use in common systems:

1) If it was a meeting (recording/transcript/notes)
- Search the transcript/notes for keywords: “Sentinel”, “Project Sentinel”, “Sentinel pilot”, customer names, or “POC”.
- If you have an audio file, run it through a transcription service (Otter, Rev, AWS Transcribe); then grep for the keywords above.

2) If it was in Salesforce / CRM
- SOQL example to find opportunities related to Sentinel:
  SELECT Account.Name, Name, StageName, CloseDate FROM Opportunity WHERE Name LIKE '%Sentinel%' OR Description LIKE '%Sentinel%' ORDER BY LastModifiedDate DESC
- Also search Accounts and Notes for the keyword “Sentinel”.

3) If it was in Slack or Teams
- Slack: search for "Project Sentinel" or just "Sentinel" and filter by date or channel. Use from:, in:channel or has:link to narrow.
- Teams: use the chat/channel search box for the same keywords.

4) If it was in email (Gmail/Outlook)
- Gmail search: subject:("Project Sentinel" OR Sentinel) OR "Project Sentinel" in:inbox
- Outlook: Search bar with the same keywords and filter by sender/meeting invite.

5) If it was in Confluence/SharePoint/Docs
- Global search for “Project Sentinel” and inspect pages/meeting notes. Look at page history/comments for names and dates.

6) If you want a checklist/template to capture the customers once you find them
- Customer name
- Primary contact (name, role, email)
- Discussion summary (interest, use case)
- Stage (Interested / Pilot / Contract / On hold)
- Next action and owner
- Target timeline

If you paste the meeting notes, email thread, or point me to the specific repository (or give me a copy), I’ll extract the customer names and produce a short table with contact, interest level and next steps. Which source do you want me to check?
